details.body:
finding_summary: Historical lineage is resolved from the live milestone plan instead of the plan hash/version captured on the task/run.
severity: medium
affected_files: ["src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs", "src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs"]
remediation_hint: Use the persisted `plan_hash`/`plan_version` (or persist a bead-title/acceptance snapshot) when filling lineage metadata, and fall back to durable IDs instead of projecting today's plan onto old runs/tasks.

Evidence: `load_task_lineage_detail` always reparses the current `plan.json` (`src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:1364-1426`), and `read_bead_lineage` / `bead_execution_history` / `list_tasks_for_milestone` do the same (`src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2065-2218`). Those lookups ignore provenance already stored on `TaskSource` and `TaskRunEntry` (`src/contexts/project_run_record/model.rs:25-41`, `src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs:364-376`). After any milestone replan or bead rename/removal, these queries will silently relabel historical tasks/runs with the current bead title and acceptance criteria, or return empty lineage details for runs that were valid under the earlier plan version.

Bug: `load_task_lineage_detail` (project_run_record/service.rs:1364-1427) propagates errors from `milestone_store.read_milestone_record()` and `plan_store.read_plan_json()` via `?`. If a project has a `task_source` pointing to a milestone whose record or plan.json has been deleted or is corrupt, `show_project` returns an error — breaking `project show` and `task show` entirely for that project. Lineage is supplemental display data; it should gracefully degrade to `Ok(None)` when the milestone/plan cannot be loaded. Fix: catch errors from the milestone/plan loading path and return `Ok(None)` instead of propagating. Affected files: src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs (load_task_lineage_detail, lines 1377-1383).

Maintainability: `load_task_lineage_detail` (project_run_record/service.rs:1386-1418) reimplements the bead-in-plan lookup logic (workstream iteration, bead ID matching with milestone prefix stripping, acceptance criteria resolution from acceptance_map) that is already factored out as `resolve_bead_plan_details` + `build_bead_lineage_view` in milestone_record/service.rs:2077-2127, and exposed via the public `read_bead_lineage` function. The two implementations could diverge if matching rules change. Fix: replace the body of `load_task_lineage_detail` with a call to `crate::contexts::milestone_record::service::read_bead_lineage(milestone_store, plan_store, base_dir, &milestone_id, &task_source.bead_id)`, which already does the same work. This also simplifies adding the graceful degradation from the first amendment. Affected files: src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs (load_task_lineage_detail).
